Title: Arabidopsis FIBRILLIN6 influences carotenoid biosynthesis by directly promoting phytoene synthase activity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photoprotection, photosynthetic pigmentation, dissipation of excess light energy, ROS detoxification
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid biosynthesis by enhancing PSY activity; loss of FBN6 attenuates carotenoid accumulation during de-etiolation and high-light acclimation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FBN6 physically interacts with PSY and co-localizes with it in specific chloroplast subdomains. 2. FBN6 specifically enhances plant PSY activity in E. coli and in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves, increasing phytoene and downstream carotenoids. 3. Arabidopsis fbn6-1 mutants show impaired carotenoid accumulation during seedling de-etiolation and after high-light exposure, demonstrating physiological relevance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-immunoprecipitation; confocal co-localization; heterologous co-expression in E. coli; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis loss-of-function mutant analysis; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; specific envelope or thylakoid membrane domains distinct from plastoglobules
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced during seedling de-etiolation; unchanged after 24 h high light relative to normal light
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced during seedling de-etiolation; unchanged after 24 h high light relative to normal light
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: De-etiolation; high light; norflurazon treatment in transient assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: fbn6-1 mutants show delayed greening, reduced chlorophyll accumulation during de-etiolation, impaired photoprotection/ROS homeostasis; attempted overexpression suggests transcript accumulation may be tightly regulated
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to boost carotenoid biosynthesis by enhancing native PSY activity, especially in plastids; likely most effective when stacked with PSY rather than used alone; expression may need tight tuning because transgenic overaccumulation appears constrained
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FBN6 had no effect on bacterial crtB activity, did not alter PSY protein accumulation or protease sensitivity, and did not change carotenoid degradation/stability in senescence assays; data support a direct positive effect on PSY enzymatic activity rather than on carotenoid sequestration or stability.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the first committed carotenoid product and supplies the precursor pool for downstream carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PSY activity increases phytoene and downstream carotenoid accumulation; its activity is promoted by FBN6
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY physically interacts with FBN6 in chloroplasts. 2. Co-expression with FBN6 increases PSY-derived phytoene and carotenoid production in bacteria and Nicotiana benthamiana leaves. 3. Arabidopsis carotenoid accumulation during de-etiolation and high light depends in part on FBN6-supported PSY activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-immunoprecipitation; confocal co-localization; heterologous enzyme activity assays in E. coli; transient expression with metabolite profiling in Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis mutant physiology
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; punctate subplastidial domains likely in envelope or thylakoid membrane regions distinct from plastoglobules
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced during de-etiolation; unchanged after 24 h high light relative to normal light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced during de-etiolation; unchanged after 24 h high light relative to normal light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: De-etiolation; high light; norflurazon treatment in transient leaf assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control and committed-step enzyme of carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Established engineering target for raising carotenoids; co-expression with FBN6 may improve flux more effectively than PSY alone
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient overexpression constructs in Nicotiana benthamiana; PSY-GFP and PSY-HA constructs; exact promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FBN6 did not increase accumulation or stability of PSY protein and did not enhance activity of bacterial crtB, indicating specificity for plant PSY.
